title_id: Hadis #33: Bukti Setelah Sumpah dan Satu Saksi dengan Sumpah Penggugat
content_en: Here in this hadith the word 'Muslim' has been stressed with a view to emphasizing the fact that, apart from other considerations, the person has no regard even for the brotherhood oflslam." 1 Cf., al-Bugha and Mistu, pp. 246-7. Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi to the one who was demanding his rights (the plaintiff). This hadith is recorded by al-Daaraqutni, al-Baihaqi and al-Haakim. However, its chain is weak1 However, the Hanafis argue that such is not the case. The plaintiff is not asked to make an oath in any of the hadith of the Prophet (peace be upon him), such as this one under discussion here.2 Evidence Presented After the Defendant Takes an Oath When the claimant does not present any proof, the case is dropped when the defendant takes an oath. On that point, there is no difference of opinion. However, there is a difference of opinion as to what should be done if the plaintiff offers proof after the defendant has already taken an oath. One opinion is that the oath is a weak method of proof and by itself it does not terminate the dispute. Therefore, if evidence is offered later, it may overturn an oath made by a defendant. It is narrated that Umar said, "A lying oath is more deserving ofrejection than trustworthy evidence." This is the view of the Hanafis, Shafiees and Hanbalis. Malik allows the entrance of new evidence after an oath by the defendant only if the plaintiff was not aware of that evidence at the time of the oath. But if the plaintiff was aware of that evidence but did not present it and, instead, preferred to put the defendant to an oath, he cannot later present that evidence. Al-Ghazzaali of the Shafiee school is also of this view. A third opinion is that the oath is decisive and no further evidence may be presented later. In other words, the case is over and decided. This is the view of the Dhaahiris. 3 It seems, Allah knows best, that the Maliki view, that only allows new evidence if the plaintiff was unaware of it previously, is the strongest in the light of the goals of the shareeah: justice and the fulfillment of rights. Deciding a Case Based on One Witness and an Oath by the Plaintiff It could be the case that the plaintiff only has one witness to support his case. However, the plaintiff may also be willing to swear an oath that what he is claiming is true. The question arises as to whether that one witness and oath would be enough to tilt the case in his favor. 1 That hadith was declared weak by ibn Hajr, al-Sanaani and al-Albaani. See Ahmad ibn Hajr, Talkhees al-Habeer fl Takhreej Ahadeeth al-Raa.fii al-Kabeer (Madinah: 1964), vol. 4, p. 209; al-Sanaani, vol. 4, p. 1948; al-Albaani, lrwa, vol. 8, pp. 267-268. Logically speaking, this author has a problem with the Shafiee view. If the defendant knows that the charges against him are not true-- implying that the plaintiff is either lying or deluded-- why would he possibly be willing to request the plaintiff to make an oath and have the decision made in his favor? Granted, this author is dealing with this issue at a theoretical level while the jurists were dealing with it at a rractical level. Hence, the logic behind such an act may have been clear to them. Cf., al-Bugha and Mistu, p. 246. 3 Cf., Mahmassani, pp. 190-192. , Hadith #33: "Were People to be Given . . . " According to the Hanafis, such is not sufficient. The plaintiff must have a "complete" evidence, meaning two witnesses. If he does not, the defendant is simply required to make an oath to clear himself. Their evidence for that position is the hadith from Sahih Muslim, "Your two witnesses or his oath." They also base it on the generality of the meaning of the hadith, "The oath is upon the defendant." (Recorded by al-Al-Al-Al-Al-Bukhari.) According to the Malikis, Shafiees and Hanbalis, a case may be decided on the basis of one witness and an oath by the plaintiff. They say that this is valid in cases related to money and wealth only. Their proof for their position is the hadith in Sahih Muslim that states that the Prophet (peace be upon him) decided a case based on one witness and an oath. This has also been reported from Abu Bakr, Ali ibn Abu Taalib and Umar ibn Abdul Azeez.1 There is no question that this hadith just referred to specifies the above hadith that seem to require two witnesses and requires an oath only on the part of the defendant. Hence, the Prophet's example must be accepted and followed in this case. 2 Furthermore, as was stated earlier, the bayyinah or evidence need not be restricted to witnesses. A person's oath may also be used to support his case.
content_id:
Bukti yang Diajukan Setelah Tergugat Bersumpah: Ketika pengklaim tidak mengajukan bukti apa pun, kasus ditutup ketika tergugat bersumpah. Mengenai poin itu, tidak ada perbedaan pendapat. Namun, terdapat perbedaan pendapat mengenai apa yang harus dilakukan jika penggugat mengajukan bukti setelah tergugat telah bersumpah.

Satu pendapat adalah bahwa sumpah adalah metode pembuktian yang lemah dan dengan sendirinya tidak mengakhiri perselisihan. Oleh karena itu, jika bukti diajukan kemudian, bukti tersebut dapat membatalkan sumpah yang dibuat oleh tergugat. Diriwayatkan bahwa Umar berkata: 'Sumpah yang bohong lebih layak untuk ditolak daripada bukti yang dapat dipercaya.' Ini adalah pandangan kaum Hanafiyah, Syafi'iyah, dan Hanabilah.

Malik memperbolehkan masuknya bukti baru setelah sumpah tergugat hanya jika penggugat tidak mengetahui bukti tersebut pada saat sumpah. Namun jika penggugat mengetahui bukti tersebut tetapi tidak mengajukannya dan, sebaliknya, lebih memilih untuk membuat tergugat bersumpah, beliau tidak dapat kemudian mengajukan bukti tersebut. Al-Ghazali dari mazhab Syafi'i juga berpandangan demikian.

Pendapat ketiga adalah bahwa sumpah itu menentukan dan tidak ada bukti lebih lanjut yang dapat diajukan kemudian. Dengan kata lain, kasus sudah selesai dan diputuskan. Ini adalah pandangan kaum Dhahiriyah. Tampaknya, wallahu a'lam, pandangan Maliki yang hanya memperbolehkan bukti baru jika penggugat sebelumnya tidak mengetahuinya, adalah yang terkuat mengingat tujuan-tujuan syariat: keadilan dan pemenuhan hak-hak.

Memutuskan Kasus Berdasarkan Satu Saksi dan Sumpah Penggugat: Mungkin saja penggugat hanya memiliki satu saksi untuk mendukung kasusnya. Namun penggugat mungkin juga bersedia bersumpah bahwa apa yang dikklaimnya adalah benar. Pertanyaannya adalah apakah satu saksi dan sumpah itu sudah cukup untuk memenangkan kasusnya.

Menurut kaum Hanafiyah, hal itu tidak cukup. Penggugat harus memiliki bukti yang 'lengkap,' artinya dua saksi. Jika tidak ada, tergugat cukup diminta bersumpah untuk membebaskan dirinya. Bukti mereka untuk posisi itu adalah hadis dari Sahih Muslim: 'Dua orang saksimu atau sumpahnya.' Mereka juga mendasarkannya pada keumuman makna hadis: 'Sumpah ada pada tergugat.' (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Menurut kaum Malikiyah, Syafi'iyah, dan Hanabilah, suatu kasus dapat diputuskan berdasarkan satu saksi dan sumpah penggugat. Mereka mengatakan bahwa ini berlaku dalam kasus-kasus yang berkaitan dengan uang dan harta saja. Bukti mereka untuk posisi ini adalah hadis dalam Sahih Muslim yang menyatakan bahwa Nabi ﷺ memutuskan suatu kasus berdasarkan satu saksi dan sumpah. Hal ini juga diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakr, Ali ibn Abi Thalib, dan Umar ibn Abdul Aziz. Selain itu, sebagaimana disebutkan sebelumnya, bayyinah atau bukti tidak perlu dibatasi pada saksi-saksi. Sumpah seseorang juga dapat digunakan untuk mendukung kasusnya.
